name: Breadboard
tagline: Solderless prototyping board for building and testing circuits without soldering
description: A breadboard is a reusable prototyping tool that lets you build circuits without soldering. It has rows of holes connected internally — power rails run along the sides and tie strips run horizontally in the middle. The standard 830-point full-size breadboard is ideal for prototyping Arduino and electronics projects at any skill level.

pinout.name: VCC
pinout.desc: Positive power rail — connect to 5V or 3.3V

pinout.name: GND
pinout.desc: Ground power rail — connect to GND

pinout.name: Tie Strip (Left)
pinout.desc: Columns a–e are connected horizontally per row

pinout.name: Tie Strip (Right)
pinout.desc: Columns f–j are connected horizontally per row (split from a–e)

wiringNote: The center divider separates the left (a–e) and right (f–j) halves. Power rails on opposite sides are NOT connected to each other unless bridged with a wire.
commonMistakes: Bridging the center gap with a component leg — left and right halves are NOT connected
Using damaged/loose holes that lose contact → intermittent faults
Not connecting both power rails when using components on both sides
Inserting ICs incorrectly across the center divider (they should straddle it)
